mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing equity valuations. A sustained downward trend in FEDFUNDS over 3 months produces positive S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days as market reprices equity risk premium downward.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.91% as of 2024-01-15 suggests accommodative stance
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS declines 3-month-over-3-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity. When UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window, equity investors reprice growth expectations downward, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.9% as of 2024-01-15 remains historically low
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, equity investors increase growth forecasts, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The interaction captures demand momentum that neither series alone can explain.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 507533M and HOUST at 1173K as of 2024-01-15
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with sustained consumer demand
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in less than 58% of instances where both RSAFS and HOUST grow month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) measures real economic capacity utilization. A sustained 2-month decline in INDPRO signals supply-side weakness and reduced corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations compress.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in less than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) and unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly capture labor market tightness. When PAYEMS growth accelerates while UNRATE remains stable or declines, wage pressure and consumer confidence increase, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The interaction reflects labor market strength that supports equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 149128K and UNRATE at 3.9% as of 2024-01-15
regime_hint: tight labor market with sustained employment growth
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in less than 56% of instances where PAYEMS growth exceeds 0.2% month-over-month and UNRATE is stable, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL above 3% combined with elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 4%) signals restrictive monetary policy. This regime produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as equity investors price in sustained real rate pressure and reduced corporate profit margins.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 251.286 and FEDFUNDS at 1.91% as of 2024-01-15
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in less than 54% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly captures aggregate demand strength. When annualized GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5%, equity investors increase earnings forecasts, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days. This always-on trend signal reflects the fundamental relationship between economic growth and equity valuations.
regime_hint: any regime with positive real growth
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in less than 54% of periods where annualized GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly signal supply-side capacity and labor utilization. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, productive capacity expansion supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The interaction captures real economic expansion that neither series alone fully captures.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 107.9989 and PAYEMS at 149128K as of 2024-01-15
regime_hint: supply-side expansion with synchronized labor and production growth
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in less than 57% of instances where both INDPRO and PAYEMS grow month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.
